nodes.name: Main MCU
nodes.description: SiP with 8MB flash + 2MB PSRAM, WiFi + BLE + Bluetooth Classic.

nodes.name: Power Management IC
nodes.description: Manages all voltage rails, LiPo charging, and USB-C power input.

nodes.name: Real-time Clock
nodes.description: Low power RTC with alarm function and backup battery input.

nodes.name: LiPo Battery
nodes.description: Single-cell 3.7V LiPo rechargeable battery.

nodes.name: USB-C Connector
nodes.description: For charging and programming ESP32.

nodes.name: Heart Rate & SpO2 Sensor
nodes.description: Integrated pulse oximetry and heart-rate sensor module.

nodes.name: 6-axis IMU
nodes.description: Accelerometer and gyroscope for motion tracking.

nodes.name: Haptic Driver
nodes.description: Haptic feedback driver for LRA motors.

nodes.name: LRA Vibration Motor
nodes.description: Linear Resonant Actuator for haptic feedback.

nodes.name: 1.54 inch IPS LCD
nodes.description: 1.54" IPS LCD with 240x240 resolution, ST7789V3 driver.

nodes.name: Capacitive Touch Controller
nodes.description: Capacitive touch overlay controller for the display.

nodes.name: PDM Microphone
nodes.description: PDM digital microphone for audio input.

nodes.name: I2S Speaker Amplifier
nodes.description: Class D I2S speaker amplifier.

nodes.name: Small Speaker
nodes.description: Small 8-10mm speaker for audio output.

nodes.name: LTE-M Cellular Module
nodes.description: LTE-M cellular module with GNSS capability.

nodes.name: LTE Module Main LDO
nodes.description: High-efficiency 3.3V LDO regulator, 2A peak current capable for SIM7080G.

nodes.name: eSIM MFF2 Package
nodes.description: MFF2 soldered eSIM for cellular connectivity.

nodes.name: GPS/GNSS Module
nodes.description: u-blox M10 standard precision GNSS module.

nodes.name: LTE-M FPC Antenna Connector
nodes.description: Connector for external LTE-M FPC antenna.

nodes.name: GPS FPC Antenna Connector
nodes.description: Connector for external GPS L1 FPC antenna (user preference).

nodes.name: POWER Button
nodes.description: Momentary switch for power on/off control.

nodes.name: BOOT Button
nodes.description: Momentary switch to enter ESP32 download mode.

nodes.name: RTC Backup Battery
nodes.description: Small coin cell battery for PCF8563 RTC backup power.

nodes.name: LTE Bulk Capacitor
nodes.description: Bulk capacitor for LTE module power stability (1000uF).

nodes.name: I2C0 Pull-ups
nodes.description: 4.7kΩ pull-up resistors for shared I2C Bus 0.

nodes.name: I2C1 Pull-ups
nodes.description: 4.7kΩ pull-up resistors for separate I2C Bus 1.

nodes.name: Smartwatch Main Enclosure
nodes.description: Main body of the smartwatch housing PCB, display, and battery. Includes internal mounts, cutouts for USB-C, mic, speaker, buttons, and a window for HR/SpO2 sensor.

nodes.name: Smartwatch Back Cover
nodes.description: Bottom cover for the smartwatch, seals the enclosure. Includes opening for heart rate sensor lens.

nodes.name: Display Retaining Frame
nodes.description: Frame to hold the 1.54 inch IPS LCD and capacitive touch panel securely in the main enclosure.

nodes.name: Power Button Actuator
nodes.description: Custom button cap for the physical power button.

nodes.name: Boot Button Actuator
nodes.description: Custom button cap for the physical boot (download mode) button.

nodes.name: HR Sensor Optical Lens
nodes.description: Transparent insert for the MAX30102 LED/sensor window, allowing skin contact while protecting the sensor.

nodes.name: PCB Mounting Standoffs
nodes.description: Small brass standoffs for mounting the PCB within the enclosure.

nodes.name: PCB Mounting Screws
nodes.description: Small screws to secure the PCB to the standoffs.

nodes.name: Enclosure Closure Screws
nodes.description: Screws to secure the back cover to the main enclosure.

nodes.name: Smartwatch Strap
nodes.description: Standard smartwatch band to wear the device. Will house external FPC antennas.

nodes.name: LTE-M FPC Antenna
nodes.description: Flexible PCB antenna for LTE-M, to be integrated into the watch band.

nodes.name: GPS FPC Antenna
nodes.description: Flexible PCB antenna for GPS L1, to be integrated into the watch band (user preference).

nodes.name: USB-C Port Gasket
nodes.description: Small silicone gasket to provide water resistance around the USB-C port.

nodes.name: Button Gaskets
nodes.description: Small silicone gaskets for water resistance around the power and boot buttons.

nodes.name: Display Mounting Adhesive
nodes.description: Double-sided adhesive tape for securing the display assembly to the enclosure.

tools: Solder paste and stencil (for QFN/BGA/0402 components)
3D printer (FDM for PETG, SLA for clear resin)
assumptions: Custom 4-layer PCB has been manufactured externally
Builder has experience with surface-mount device (SMD) soldering, including fine-pitch and QFN/BGA packages
Basic familiarity with ESP32 firmware development (ESP-IDF or Arduino IDE)
Access to 3D printing resources for specified materials and settings
Understanding of I2C, SPI, UART, I2S, and PDM communication protocols

subSteps.title: 3D print all PETG enclosure parts
3D print HR sensor optical lens from clear resin
Clean and deburr all 3D printed components
Inspect custom PCB for manufacturing defects
Solder ESP32-PICO-V3-02 and AXP2101 to PCB
Solder LTE (SIM7080G, LDO, bulk capacitor, eSIM, FPC connector) and GPS (MIA-M10Q, FPC connector) modules/components
Solder I2C (PCF8563, BMI270, MAX30102, DRV2605L, FT6336U), SPI (ST7789V3), and audio (SPM1423HM4H-B, MAX98357A) components
Solder USB-C connector, power/boot buttons, and passives (0402 resistors, capacitors)
Connect LRA vibration motor and small speaker to their respective drivers on the PCB
Connect LiPo battery to AXP2101 input pads and coin cell battery to PCF8563 input pads
Perform initial power-on via USB-C and verify AXP2101 operation and voltage rails
Connect ESP32 via USB-C and verify basic connectivity and flash initial bootloader/firmware
Test all I2C buses (Bus 0 & Bus 1) to confirm recognition of AXP2101, PCF8563, BMI270, MAX30102, DRV2605L, and FT6336U
Test SPI display (ST7789V3) functionality and confirm image output and backlight control
Verify PDM microphone (SPM1423HM4H-B) input and I2S speaker amplifier (MAX98357A) output
Initiate communication with SIM7080G LTE module and MIA-M10Q GPS module via UART
Mount PCB into the main enclosure using standoffs and screws

detail.summary: Mount the main PCB into the smartwatch enclosure with standoffs and screws.
steps: Carefully position the assembled 38x34mm PCB inside the 3D-printed Smartwatch Main Enclosure.
Align the PCB's mounting holes with the four integrated mounting points in the enclosure.
Insert four M1.4x3mm PCB Mounting Standoffs through the PCB's mounting holes into the enclosure.
Secure the PCB by fastening four M1.4x2mm PCB Mounting Screws into the standoffs, ensuring a snug fit.
detail.tip: Avoid overtightening the tiny screws to prevent damage to the PCB or 3D-printed enclosure mounts.

subSteps.title: Install USB-C port gasket and waterproof button gaskets, then insert button actuators
Secure the display (ST7789V3) and touch panel (FT6336U) into the retaining frame, then attach to the main enclosure with adhesive tape
Place the LiPo battery within the enclosure, stacked as specified
Insert the HR sensor optical lens into the back cover, then secure the back cover to the main enclosure
Integrate LTE and GPS FPC antennas into the smartwatch strap and connect to FPC connectors on PCB
Attach the completed watch case to the smartwatch strap
Perform final system test of all functions

projectDescription: A 38x34mm ESP32-PICO-V3-02-based smartwatch prototype integrating a 1.54" IPS LCD with touch, HR/SpO2, IMU, PDM mic, and LTE-M/GPS for comprehensive wearable functionality. Power is managed by an AXP2101, supporting LiPo battery via USB-C.
originalPrompt:
Design a smartwatch prototype PCB based on ESP32 with the following specifications:

## MCU & Core
- Main MCU: ESP32-PICO-V3-02 (SiP with 8MB flash + 2MB PSRAM, WiFi + BLE + Bluetooth Classic)
- Power management: AXP2101 (I2C address 0x34) managing all voltage rails
- Real-time clock: PCF8563 (I2C address 0x51) with backup coin cell battery
- Battery: single-cell 3.7V LiPo ~300-400mAh, USB-C charging

## Sensors (all on shared I2C bus, SDA/SCL with 4.7kΩ pull-ups)
- Heart rate + SpO2: MAX30102 (I2C address 0x57) — place on board edge for skin contact with LED window cutout
- IMU 6-axis (accelerometer + gyroscope): BMI270 (I2C address 0x68)
- Haptic feedback: DRV2605L (I2C address 0x5A) driving a LRA vibration motor

## Display
- 1.54" IPS LCD 240x240, driver ST7789V3, SPI interface (CS, MOSI, SCK, DC pins)
- Capacitive touch overlay: FT6336U (I2C address 0x38) on a SEPARATE I2C bus (Wire1)
- Backlight controlled via AXP2101 ALDO2 rail

## Audio
- PDM microphone: SPM1423HM4H-B (CLK + DATA, 2 GPIOs)
- I2S speaker amplifier: MAX98357A (BCLK, WCLK, DOUT — 3 GPIOs) driving a small 8-10mm speaker

## Wireless communication
- WiFi 802.11 b/g/n + BLE 5.0 + Bluetooth Classic A2DP (all handled by ESP32)
- LTE-M cellular: SIM7080G module connected via UART (TX, RX) with separate 3.3V LDO regulator capable of 2A peak current, plus a 1000µF bulk capacitor
- eSIM: MFF2 package soldered near the SIM7080G SIM interface
- GPS/GNSS: u-blox MIA-M10Q connected via UART (TX, RX)

## Antennas
- PCB trace antenna (IFA/PIFA) for WiFi/Bluetooth with proper ground plane clearance
- FPC antenna connector for LTE-M (external FPC antenna in watch band)
- Ceramic chip antenna or shared with LTE for GPS L1

## Power rails (managed by AXP2101)
- DC1: 3.3V → ESP32
- ALDO2: backlight
- ALDO3: display + touch
- ALDO4: LTE module enable
- BLDO1: GPS module enable
- BLDO2: DRV2605 enable
- Separate LDO (e.g. TPS62743 or AP2112): LTE module main power (2A peak capable)

## Physical constraints
- Target PCB size: 38mm x 34mm (Apple Watch form factor)
- 4-layer PCB (signal — GND — power — signal)
- Components: 0402 passives minimum, QFN/BGA packages where needed
- USB-C connector for charging and programming
- 2 buttons: POWER (long press on/off) + BOOT (enter download mode)

## I2C bus summary
- Bus 0 (Wire): SDA/SCL → AXP2101 (0x34), PCF8563 (0x51), BMI270 (0x68), MAX30102 (0x57), DRV2605 (0x5A)
- Bus 1 (Wire1): SDA1/SCL1 → FT6336U touch (0x38)

Generate: schematic, component placement suggestion, and bill of materials.
plan:
- ESP32-PICO-V3-02 SiP, AXP2101 PMIC, PCF8563 RTC, LiPo battery, USB-C for core power and charging.
- MAX30102 HR/SpO2, BMI270 IMU, DRV2605L haptic driver with LRA motor, SPM1423HM4H-B PDM mic, MAX98357A I2S speaker amp for sensors and audio.
- 1.54" IPS LCD (ST7789V3) with FT6336U capacitive touch, controlled via SPI and a separate I2C bus.
- SIM7080G LTE-M module with dedicated LDO and FPC antenna connector, MIA-M10Q GPS module with ceramic antenna, eSIM for cellular connectivity.
- Custom 4-layer PCB (38mm x 34mm) with integrated WiFi/BT PCB antenna, 0402 passives, and QFN/BGA packages.
- USB-C, POWER button, BOOT button for user interface and programming.
